questions.q: What celestial body is the Aditya-L1 mission designed to study?
questions.explanation: Aditya-L1 is India's first dedicated solar observatory, built to study the Sun.

questions.q: Which Indian space agency developed and launched Aditya-L1?
questions.explanation: Aditya-L1 was developed and launched by the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO).

questions.q: In which year was Aditya-L1 launched?
questions.explanation: Aditya-L1 was launched on 2 September 2023.

questions.q: The 'L1' in Aditya-L1 refers to a special point in space called a what?
questions.explanation: L1 refers to the first Sun-Earth Lagrange point, where the spacecraft is stationed.

questions.q: What does the word 'Aditya' mean, reflecting the mission's target?
questions.explanation: 'Aditya' is a Sanskrit name for the Sun, fitting for India's solar mission.

questions.q: Aditya-L1 is best described as India's first dedicated what?
questions.explanation: Aditya-L1 is India's first dedicated solar observatory spacecraft.

questions.q: Around which point does Aditya-L1 orbit to observe the Sun continuously?
questions.explanation: It orbits the Sun-Earth L1 Lagrange point, about 1.5 million km from Earth.

questions.q: A key advantage of placing Aditya-L1 at L1 is that it can view the Sun how?
questions.explanation: From L1, the spacecraft has a continuous, unobstructed view of the Sun without being blocked by Earth.

questions.q: Aditya-L1 was launched from which Indian spaceport?
questions.explanation: It was launched from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre at Sriharikota.

questions.q: Aditya-L1 is a mission to study a star. Which star is that?
questions.explanation: Aditya-L1 studies our own Sun, the nearest star to Earth.

questions.q: Which launch vehicle carried Aditya-L1 into space?
questions.explanation: Aditya-L1 was launched aboard the PSLV-C57 mission.

questions.q: Approximately how far from Earth is the Sun-Earth L1 point where Aditya-L1 is stationed?
questions.explanation: The L1 point lies about 1.5 million km from Earth toward the Sun, roughly 1% of the Earth-Sun distance.

questions.q: Which is the primary visible-light coronagraph payload aboard Aditya-L1?
questions.explanation: The Visible Emission Line Coronagraph (VELC) is the primary instrument, studying the solar corona.

questions.q: How many scientific payloads does Aditya-L1 carry?
questions.explanation: Aditya-L1 carries seven scientific payloads to study the Sun and its environment.

questions.q: The SUIT payload on Aditya-L1 images the Sun in which part of the spectrum?
questions.explanation: The Solar Ultraviolet Imaging Telescope (SUIT) images the Sun in near-ultraviolet light.

questions.q: On which date did Aditya-L1 successfully reach and enter its halo orbit around L1?
questions.explanation: Aditya-L1 was inserted into its halo orbit around the L1 point on 6 January 2024.

questions.q: Several Aditya-L1 payloads study which faint outer layer of the Sun's atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Coronagraphs like VELC study the corona, the Sun's faint, extremely hot outer atmosphere.

questions.q: Studying which solar phenomena, which can disrupt satellites and power grids, is a key goal of Aditya-L1?
options: Coronal mass ejections and space weather
questions.explanation: A major goal is understanding coronal mass ejections and space weather that affect technology on and near Earth.

questions.q: What type of orbit around L1 does Aditya-L1 occupy?
questions.explanation: Aditya-L1 occupies a halo orbit around the L1 Lagrange point.

questions.q: The ASPEX and PAPA payloads aboard Aditya-L1 are primarily designed to study what?
options: The solar wind and energetic particles
questions.explanation: ASPEX and PAPA are in-situ instruments that measure the solar wind plasma and energetic particles at L1.

questions.q: Why is the L1 Lagrange point especially useful for a continuous solar mission?
options: Gravitational forces let a spacecraft stay roughly fixed relative to Sun and Earth
It is the closest point to the Sun
questions.explanation: At L1 the gravitational pulls of the Sun and Earth balance with orbital motion, letting the craft maintain a stable, fuel-efficient position for uninterrupted solar viewing.

questions.q: Aditya-L1 was India's first space-based mission dedicated to studying which object, following missions to the Moon and Mars?
questions.explanation: After the Chandrayaan (Moon) and Mangalyaan (Mars) missions, Aditya-L1 became India's first dedicated solar mission.

questions.q: The lead institute that developed the VELC coronagraph for Aditya-L1 is the Indian Institute of Astrophysics, headquartered in which city?
questions.explanation: The Indian Institute of Astrophysics (IIA), based in Bengaluru, led the development of the VELC payload.

questions.q: A distinctive feature of VELC is that it performs internal occultation to image the corona starting very close to the solar disc, at roughly how many solar radii?
questions.explanation: VELC can observe the corona as close as about 1.05 solar radii from the Sun's centre, closer in than many other coronagraphs.

questions.q: Which Aditya-L1 payload is designed to measure the magnetic field of the spacecraft's environment at L1?
questions.explanation: The onboard Magnetometer (MAG) measures the interplanetary magnetic field at the L1 point.

questions.q: The HEL1OS and SoLEXS payloads on Aditya-L1 are spectrometers used to study solar flares in which energy band?
questions.explanation: SoLEXS (soft X-ray) and HEL1OS (hard X-ray) monitor solar flares in the X-ray band.

questions.q: What does the abbreviation VELC, the name of Aditya-L1's primary payload, stand for?
questions.explanation: VELC stands for Visible Emission Line Coronagraph, the mission's main coronal instrument.

questions.q: Roughly how long did Aditya-L1 take to travel from launch to its insertion into the halo orbit around L1?
questions.explanation: Launched on 2 September 2023 and inserted on 6 January 2024, the cruise took roughly 125 days.

questions.q: The PAPA payload aboard Aditya-L1 specifically analyses which component of the solar wind?
questions.explanation: PAPA (Plasma Analyser Package for Aditya) measures the composition and energy of solar wind electrons and ions.

questions.q: One scientific aim of Aditya-L1 is to help explain the long-standing 'coronal heating problem.' What does this problem concern?
options: Why the corona is far hotter than the Sun's visible surface
Why the Sun is cooling down
Why the Sun has an 11-year cycle
questions.explanation: The coronal heating problem is the puzzle of why the corona reaches millions of degrees while the photosphere below is only about 5,500 degrees Celsius.
